semanticCore: Punching, striking with the fist, pushing away violently, or thrusting with a staff

keyDerivations.meaning: To strike with the fist, punch, push away with force, or jab

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَوَكَزَهُ مُوسَىٰ فَقَضَىٰ عَلَيْهِ (28:15): So Moses struck him with his fist, and it ended him (killed him).
discardedNoise: Wakkaz (walking stick/crutch) - related noun but secondary here.
summaryEn: The root represents striking with a closed fist (Wakz), recounting the incident where Moses accidentally killed an Egyptian with a single punch while defending an Israelite.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى ضرب الشخص بجمع الكف والبوكس ودفع الصدر بقوة (وكز)، واصفاً ضربة موسى القوية للفرعوني بجمع يده التي أدت لوفاته خطأً.
rawText: مُتَوَكِّزٌ بأَمْرٍ Standing ready, or prepared, for a thing, or an affair. (L, art. وكد.) وَكَزَهُ 1 , (S, Msb,) aor. يَكِزُهُ, (Msb,) inf. n. وَكْزٌ, (Msb, K,) He struck, or beat him, (S, Msb,) [with anything,] as, for ex., with a staff, or stick: (TA:) or it signifies, (Msb,) or signifies also (S) he struck, or beat, him with his fist upon his chin: (S, Msb:) or, accord. to Ks, i. q. لَكَمَهُ; (Msb;) [i. e.] he struck, or beat, him with his fist. (A, K.) === He pushed, or impelled, or repelled, him. (S, Msb, K.) === He pierced him (Ks, K, * TK) with a spear. (TK.) === He goaded him. (TA.) === He broke his nose. (T, TA.) وَكَّازٌ One who strikes, or beats, much with his fist. (A.) وَكْزَةٌ A blow with the fist. (A.)
